subject: A slender serpentine dragon rising from the surface of a loch, wings flared, neck arched in a sinuous S-curve with water sheeting off its body
description: A long-necked, eel-bodied dragon erupts from the still waters of a highland loch, its dark blue-black scales glistening as water cascades from its underbelly and tail. The wings are unfurled mid-shake, translucent and ribbed, catching cool backlight. The creature's pose is a graceful S-curve — more sea-serpent than bulky war-dragon — with a small horned head turned in profile. Behind it, the background is a wash of pale grey-white mist hinting at distant cliffs or shore. The water below ripples outward from the dragon's emergence point. The whole composition reads cold, quiet, Loch-Ness-mythic.
foreground: serpentine dragon mid-emergence from water, wings spread, water streaming off scales
background: pale misty atmosphere over a still loch, hints of distant shoreline
emotion: alert, predatory, emerging with quiet menace
